note: This is a Sprint weekend — no FP2/FP3. Only one practice session. The Sprint race is Saturday.

sessions.description: The only free practice session of the weekend. Teams have just 60 minutes to gather data before Sprint Qualifying — no second bite at the apple.
F2 qualifying session sets the grid for Saturday's Sprint race.
Porsche's one-make 911 GT3 Cup series, second practice session before qualifying.

Sets the grid for Saturday's Sprint race. Three knock-out rounds (SQ1, SQ2, SQ3) determine the top 8 starting positions for the 19-lap Sprint. High stakes — mistakes mean starting at the back of the Sprint.
McLaren Artura Trophy EVO one-make series, second practice session.
Sets the grid for Saturday's and Sunday's Porsche races.
Short reverse-grid sprint race for F2 — fast, frantic, and a great warm-up for the F1 action.

19 laps. Full points on offer (half of grand prix points). No mandatory pit stop. Teams can't share data between this and the grand prix setup. Fastest 8 from Sprint Qualifying get to fight for the win — expect aggressive racing and risk-taking.
Cadillac showcasing their new F1 car — a historic moment for the American manufacturer in front of a home(ish) crowd.
Sets the grid for both Sunday races.
First of two races for Porsche's one-make 911 GT3 Cup series. Expect close racing — the cars are identical, so it all comes down to driver talent.
Sets the Grand Prix grid. Three knock-out sessions (Q1: all 22 cars → eliminate 5, Q2: 17 cars → eliminate 7, Q3: top 10 shootout). Pole position at Miami is a significant advantage.
First McLaren Artura Trophy race of the weekend. 40 minutes + 1 lap.
Second and final McLaren Trophy race of the Miami weekend. 40 minutes + 1 lap.
Exclusive track experience for Precision Drive Club members.
Second and final Porsche Carrera Cup race of the weekend. Championship points on the line.
The main F2 event — full points, mandatory pit stop, reverse grid top 10. F2 Feature Races regularly produce drama and upsets that directly affect the championship.
All 22 drivers parade the circuit — your best chance for a close look at the grid before the race.
Pre-race entertainment on the grid.
Pre-race ceremony.

57 laps, 308.326 km. The main event. Broadcast on Apple TV and F1 TV Pro. Miami International Autodrome's combination of tight infield sections and sweeping outer loop creates a unique challenge for strategy and overtaking.

broadcast: Apple TV and F1 TV Pro
